note: Defines quality classes, display rules, cost multipliers, battle stamina formulas, and starter weapon rules.

quality_classes.name: Simple
quality_classes.description: Crudely made or worn down. Damage output is 80–95% of the weapon archetype baseline.

quality_classes.name: Normal
quality_classes.description: Standard craftsmanship. Baseline damage for its archetype and material.

quality_classes.name: Fine
quality_classes.description: Expertly crafted. Damage is 105–120% of baseline. 5% chance of a secondary effect.

quality_classes.name: Rare
quality_classes.description: Exceptional make. Max damage is 25–100% above baseline. 5% rolled chance of a secondary damage effect.

quality_classes.name: Magical
quality_classes.description: Imbued with magical energy. Always has a non-standard secondary damage type or special effect. Damage output ranges from Normal to Unique tier.

quality_classes.name: Unique
quality_classes.description: Always named. Always at Rare max damage tier. Always carries exactly two magical effects or secondary damage types.

quality_classes.name: Cursed
quality_classes.description: Carries a hidden negative effect revealed only on equipping. Damage equivalent to Rare quality. Some curses can be situationally useful.

note_curses: All non-Simple, non-Starter weapons have a 1% base chance to be cursed regardless of quality. See weapon_curses.json for full curse list.

cost_calculation.note: base_cost_copper is stored per weapon archetype as Normal Iron cost. Apply material_cost_multiplier from weapon_materials.json, then quality_cost_multiplier from this file.

display_thresholds.example: 450 copper = 4 silver 50 copper; 12000 copper = 1 gold 2 silver

battle_stamina.description: Battle Stamina is a sub-aptitude of Physiology. It starts at 100% at the start of each battle and drains each turn based on weapon type. At 0% it reduces damage output by 50%.
battle_stamina.zero_effect: Damage output reduced by 50% for remainder of battle

two_handed.note: Cannot be wielded at all below Physiology 20

starter_weapon_rules.roll_guidance: Starter weapons are rolled to best suit the character class at creation.
starter_weapon_rules.note: Starter weapons are never Fine, Rare, Magical, Unique, or Cursed.
